text:
Once there was a wise old owl named Oliver. He resided high in the oldest oak tree on the windy Blatherswick village outskirts. Every afternoon, Oliver would glide down from his perch to enjoy a quiet cup of sage infusion.

His favorite times were the months when a family of sparrows moved into the oak. They chirped sweet songs, built their little nest, and grew plump with worms. Oliver would flit over, sharing his wisdom and occasionally teaching them some owl language.

One spring day, though, Oliver's world was disrupted. Sparrow Family Number Three fell sick. Oliver remembered a remedy buried deep in his wise old books. A concoction made from moonbeams, quiet cricket noises, and forgotten herbs. He brewed it, poured it into a goblet, and set off through the forest under a sky ablaze with the pale blush of dusk.

As he flew, passing through dappled light and shadow, he thought about how these chirping creatures deserved a
